observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) Municipal Accountant
prepare and submit BRS on a monthly basis per bank account in accordance with
Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 and Chapter 21 of the
GAM for LGU, Volume I; and b) Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all monthly
bank statements and supporting documents (e.g., debit/credit memos) are provided
to the Municipal Accountant to expedite reconciliation; and c) both the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer strengthen coordination between the
Accounting and Treasury Departments by implementing measures to facilitate the
timely sharing of financial records, particularly bank statements, to ensure accurate
and prompt preparation of BRS for all bank accounts.

2.7 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer,
Accountant and MPDO (a) reassess its financial strategy and prioritize the
reallocation and utilization of funds totaling ₱5,407,709.87, directing these resources
toward programs/projects that would directly benefit its constituents; and (b) develop
a clear project prioritization framework to ensure that available funds are allocated
to the most critical development initiatives. This framework should consider the
urgency and impact of each project on the locality.

3.4 We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) Municipal Accountant
continue to (i) issue demand letters to all concerned AOs for them to liquidate their
cash advances by submitting complete and valid settlement documents or refunding
applicable portion of the cash advances, as necessary and (ii) monitor the schedule of
cash advances granted to different Accountable Officers to know when these cash
advances are due for liquidation. We also recommended that (b) Management stop
the practice of granting cash advances to (i) elected officials, except for their official
traveling expenses; and (ii) employees and officials with outstanding/unliquidated
cash advances; (c) SDOs for special purpose and time bound activity (i) liquidate their
cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served,
esspecially the unliquidated cash advance for the Kasalang Bayan amounting to
₱38,980.00 which is outstanding for more than three years; (ii) to be prudent in
programming their cash advances and exclude expenses which can be normally
coursed through the regular procurement process; and (d) Municipal Accountant (i)
include in the CDJ the liquidations from Advances to SDO Account; (ii) post timely
in the GL the liquidations from Advances to Officers and Employees and Advances
to SDO Accounts; and (iii) reconcile the Report of Cash Advances and the GL on a
monthly basis.

4.3 We recommended and Management agreed that the:
(a) Municipal Treasurer (i) furnish the updated RPTAR/ Taxpayer’s index card to
Municipal Accountant at every beginning of the year for recording and adjustment
purposes; (ii) issue corresponding SEF ORs and RCD for SET Income collected by
the PGT; (iii) ensure correct and timely remittance of Barangays’ share in RPT
corresponding to RPT Income collected by the PGT; (iv) transfer to the SEF the
unremitted balance of Due to Other Funds amounting ₱15,779.74; (v) be more
meticulous in preparing the Abstract of Collections and check whether the formulas
in the worksheet are correct; and (vi) intensify and strategize tax collection efforts
from taxpayers such as deployment of demand letters, posting of notice of
delinquency in conspicuous places and tapping the assistance of the barangay officials
for tax campaign to reach out to a larger area; and
(b) Municipal Accountant (i) establish RPT and SET receivables sourced from the list
duly certified by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year; (ii) exclude those
who made advance payments during the previous year in the total amount to be
established as RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; (iii) monitor the
unearned revenue for collections from advance payments in the previous year and
make necessary adjustments to recognize the realized/earned revenue during the
current year; (iv) be extra careful in recording collections from RPT/SET especially
when using the Abstract of Collections as reference; (v) record properly the share of
the Barangay from the RPT collections made by the PGT and credit the same upon
remittance of the Treasury Office; (vi) debit the Deferred RPT and credit the RPT
Receivables Accounts for the total amount of collections made by the PGT in behalf
of the Municipality; (vii) make the necessary adjustments to correct all the
aforementioned misstatements; and (viii) align the accounting procedures being
adopted with the procedures provided by the MNGAS for LGUs with respect to
recording of RPT/SET transactions.

5.8 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant to
(i) perform detailed reconciliations of all receivable accounts, particularly those due
from NGAs (₱12,024.46) and LGUs (₱121,165.18); (ii) establish a periodic review and
validation of receivable accounts to promptly identify discrepancies or unsupported
balances; (ii) henceforth, consider the write-off of dormant receivables for more than
10 years in the books of accounts, in accordance with the procedures set forth by COA
Circular No. 2016- 005; and (iii) ensure that all material and relevant information
pertaining to the Receivables account is clearly and adequately disclosed in the Notes
to the Financial Statement, in compliance with the requirements of IPSAS 1.

We recommended to (a) continue to capacitate the GFPS and concerned officials with the
mechanisms and processes in mainstreaming GAD perspective in the LGU by being well-
informed and updated of GAD-related issuances and/or attending training, conferences,
symposia administered by the PCW, DILG, or other NGAs directly involved in GAD
concerns; (b) GFPS (i) continuously update its GAD database and identify and correct the
errors in the source data; (ii) enhance the GPB and AR by completely filling out the
needed qualitative and quantitative aspects of the activities implemented including data on
sex-disaggregation pursuant to MCW, as necessary; and (iii) equip themselves with the
GeRL/HGDG tool and PIMME to determine how much of the Agency’s actual
disbursements for implementing its major programs can be attributed to GAD.

23.4 We recommended and the LCE concurred that the Municipal Accountant and other
concerned personnel enforce the immediate settlement of disallowances in
accordance with Section 9.4 of the 2009 RRSA.

We recommend and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed for the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) submit BRS on a monthly basis per bank account in accordance with Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 and Chapter 21 of the GAM for LGU, Volume I; (ii) observe proper accounting treatment in recognizing stale checks; (iii) prepare an adjusting entry for the Cash-in-Bank account and recognize back the Accounts Payable related to the stale checks in the aggregate amount of ₱156,810.08; and (iv) effectively utilize the funds totaling ₱5,307,404.91 from the time deposit and high-yield savings accounts of the Municipality towards programs/projects that would redound to the benefit of the constituents; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to monitor the checks drawn against the bank accounts of the Municipality and observe sending written notice to the payees informing them of the existence of a check at least one month before they become stale.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation and the LCE agreed to enjoin the Municipal Accountant to (i) exhaust all remedies to gather the supporting documents needed for the receivables noted and (ii) henceforth, consider the write-off of dormant receivables for more than 10 years in the books of accounts, in accordance with the procedures set forth by COA Circular No. 2016- 005.

We reiterate our prior year’s audit recommendation and the LCE agreed to enjoin the Municipal Accountant to continue to (i) issue demand letters to all concerned Aos for them to liquidate their cash advances by submitting complete and valid settlement documents or refunding applicable portion of the cash advances, as necessary and (ii) monitor the schedule of cash advances granted to different Accountable Officers to know when these cash advances are due for liquidation.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the Accounting Office and the Supply Officer, as the case may be, to: (i) maintain the Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards for all LGU inventories and observe the periodic reconciliation of the two records to monitor agency assets properly; (ii) conduct a complete semi-annual physical count of inventories and submit the corresponding RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year in accordance with Section 124 of the MNGAs for LGUs; (iii) maintain a Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to serve as a basis for preparing Journal Entries to record the supplies and materials issued; and (iv) conform with the implementing guidelines of COA Circular 2022-004 regarding the issuance of ICS.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the Inventory Committee, Accounting Office and the Property Officer, as the case may be, to: (i) conduct a complete annual physical count of PPEs and come up with the RPCPPE in accordance with Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs; (ii) maintain the Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards for all LGUs properties and observe the periodic reconciliation of the two records to monitor agency’s assets properly; (iii) maintain an Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to properly document and track the unserviceable assets of the Municipality.

We recommended and the LCE agreed for the Accounting Section maintain and regularly update the CIPLC for all ongoing construction projects of the Municipality to ensure efficient asset management and enhance financial reporting accuracy.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation and the LCE agreed to enjoin the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and maintain SLs per creditor as necessary to support the balances presented in the GL and financial statements; and (ii) exhaust all remedies to gather the details and supporting documents needed for the identification of the amounts of dormant and long-outstanding payables to facilitate the reversion and/or write-off of such in accordance with the provisions of COA-DBM JC No. 1, s. 2021 and COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to continue (i) to instruct the process owners of the Local Roads Asset Management System based on COA Circular 2015-008 to be mindful of their duties laid down in the said Circular; (ii) require the Property Officer to come up with the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads under the Agency’s jurisdiction; (iii) require the Accounting Office to disclose information on Road Networks in the Notes to the FS in accordance with COA Circular 2015-008; and (iv) ensure the maintenance and improvement of the Local Road Inventory and Road Map System.

We recommended and the LCE agreed for the Municipal Accountant to a) maintain the punctual submission of documents relating to CF; and b) submit all the documents provided in Sections 6.1.7 and 6.1.8 of the DBM, DILG, DND, GCG, and COA JC No. 2015-01.

We recommended and the LCE agreed (a) to continue upholding its mandate in promoting local development by appropriating 20% of its annual NTA pursuant to the provisions of the LGC and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and JMC No. 01 dated November 04, 2020; for the (b) Engineering Office and MPDO ensure timely delivery of the projects for CY 2023 and for every budget year, taking into account the legal rudiments of every project; (c) the Local Development Council (LDC), (i) continue to give priority to projects focusing on inclusive growth and sustainable development of communities at the barangay level; and (ii) maintain the proactive involvement of the barangays thru the LDC in identifying socio-economic projects to be implemented out of the DF.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) BAC and TWG to (i) submit the lacking documents as enumerated in the discussion; (ii) prepare the BAC resolution declaring Single Calculated and Responsive Bidder (SCRB) if there is only one responsive bidder as provided by Section 36 of IRR of R.A. 9184; (iii) ensure that all required postings in PhilGEPS as mentioned in the Revised IRR are complied within the period prescribed. Moreover, attach a printed copy of all postings to the related disbursement vouchers; (iv) conduct the post-qualification exercise through significant verification based on the documents under examination so as to establish the integrity of documents and to detect misrepresentation; (b) Engineering Office to ensure that project contractors observe the proper size and details to be provided on project signboards including the update thereof to reflect the actual project status in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and (c) instruct the Accounting Office to check and ensure the completeness of documents and exert the proper diligence to discern that the documents submitted by the contractors are legitimate before effecting payment.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the: a) Municipal Accountant to continue to (i) make necessary corrections/adjustments in affected inventory and expense accounts; (ii) observe the perpetual method in recognizing inventory and avoid recording outright expense when accounting for inventoriable goods and DRRM supplies; (iii) continue to utilize the LDRRMF only for expenses projects that are directly related to disaster risk reduction and preparation/prevention in accordance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013; (iv) discuss and present the minimum required disclosure in the Notes to the FS as specified in COA Circular No. 2014-002; and (v) revert and transfer the amount of ₱3,053,990.77 under the Special Trust Fund to General Fund; and b) Supply Officer, in coordination with concerned offices, to maintain Stock Cards and prepare and submit the RIS, SSMI and Inventory List of Items for DRRM to the Municipal Accountant for recording and adjusting related expense and inventory accounts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) members of the BAC/TWG (i) to adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR as to the completeness of documents in executing government contracts from requisition, bidding up to award; and (ii) submit the lacking documents presented from the table; and (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment.

We recommended and the LCE concurred that Management (a) direct the MSWMB to (i) to prioritize the review/update of the SWMP to ensure its sustainability, viability and effectiveness; (ii) prepare Long-term Action Plan and Annual Work Plan and Budget showing the SWM activities to be implemented and the financial counterpart for each SWM activity; and (iii) continue to improve the work of the monitoring and evaluation team and require them to prepare Progress Reports in line with the Annual Work Plan and the SWM Plan; and (b) provide the necessary support to implement the additional methods of processing waste materials in the MRF to further reduce residual wastes.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that Management through the LCE (a) continuously and constantly monitor, intensify and strategize collection efforts from taxpayers such as sending payment advisory or notice to taxpayers before the payment period or deadlines and the like; and (b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to (i) include copy of Certified Income Tax Return in the list of required documents for the application for business permit, as required by the Municipal Revenue Code; (ii) request from the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) the list of all BIR registered business establishments in San Clemente, Tarlac with their declared gross sales before the start of the licensing period; (iii) conduct the examination of books of accounts and pertinent records in accordance with Section 171 of the LGC, especially businesses/taxpayers who failed to submit their ITR; (iv) submit as soon as possible the unsubmitted ORs listed in these report; and (c) issue stern warning and possible subsequent actions against the Municipal Treasurer by the LGU if the issue on missing ORs persist and not acted swiftly.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (i) continue to remit promptly and intact the loan amortization and mandatory contributions to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth within the prescribed period; (ii) exert extra effort in gathering pertinent documents and analyze the subject accounts to establish the accuracy of the balances in the inter-agency payables; and (iii) remit all the due premiums immediately, when evidence warrants.
We recommended and the LCE concurred that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel enforce the immediate settlement of disallowances in accordance with Section 9.4 of the 2009 RRSA.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Accounting Office and the Property Officer/Municipal Treasurer, as the case may be, to: i) create an Inventory Committee and conduct a complete annual physical count of PPEs and Inventories to come up with the RPCPPE and RPCI with its related reconciliation as required by Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs; ii) maintain the Supplies/Property Ledger Cards and Stock/Property Cards, respectively, for all LGU properties; and iii) observe the periodic reconciliation of the two records to monitor agency assets properly.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to enjoin the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and maintain SLs per creditor to support the balances presented in the GL and FS; and (ii) exhaust all remedies to fully gather the details and supporting documents needed for the identification of the amounts of dormant and long-outstanding payables to facilitate the reversion and/or write-off of such in accordance with the provisions of COA-DBM JC No. 1, s. 2021 and COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the LCE agreed to require the (i) BAC and Technical Working Group (TWG) to submit the lacking documents enumerated in the discussion; (ii) Engineering Office to ensure that project contractors observe the proper size and details to be provided on project signboards including the update thereof to reflect the actual project status in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and (iii) Accounting Office to check and ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to (a) direct the MSWMB to continue to (i) review every two years or as the need arises, SWMP for the purpose of ensuring its sustainability, viability, and effectiveness; (ii) prepare an Annual Work Plan and Budget showing the SWM activities to be implemented and the financial counterpart for each SWM activity, taking into consideration the initiatives identified in the 10-year SWM Plan; and (iii) improve the work of the monitoring and evaluation team and require them to prepare Progress Reports in line with the Annual Work Plan and the SWM Plan; (b) consider reinforcing the MENRO to carve out more progress in the implementation of SWMP; and (c) require the cooperation of the Barangays in the implementation of Solid Waste Management particularly the establishment of functional MRF and campaign for proper waste segregation.

1.6 We recommend and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed for the (a)
Municipal Accountant to (i) submit BRS on a monthly basis per bank account in
accordance with Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 and
Chapter 21 of the GAM for LGU, Volume I; (ii) observe proper accounting treatment
in recognizing stale checks; (iii) prepare an adjusting entry for the Cash-in-Bank
account and recognize back the Accounts Payable related to the stale checks in the
aggregate amount of ₱156,810.08; and (iv) effectively utilize the funds totaling
₱5,307,404.91 from the time deposit and high-yield savings accounts of the
Municipality towards programs/projects that would redound to the benefit of the
constituents; and (b) Municipal Treasurer monitor the checks drawn against the
bank accounts of the Municipality and observe sending written notice to the payees
informing them of the existence of a check at least one month before they become
stale.

2.7 We reiterate our prior year’s audit recommendation and the LCE agreed to
enjoin the Municipal Accountant to (i) exhaust all remedies to gather the supporting
documents needed for the receivables noted and (ii) henceforth, consider the write-
off of dormant receivables for more than 10 years in the books of accounts, in
accordance with the procedures set forth by COA Circular No. 2016- 005.

3.5 We reiterate our prior year’s audit recommendation and the LCE agreed to
instruct the Municipal Accountant to continue to (i) issue demand letters to all
concerned Aos for them to liquidate their cash advances by submitting complete and
valid settlement documents or refunding applicable portion of the cash advances, as
necessary and (ii) monitor the schedule of cash advances granted to different
Accountable Officers to know when these cash advances are due for liquidation.

4.13 We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the Accounting Office
and the Supply Officer, as the case may be, to: (i) maintain the Stock Cards and
Supplies Ledger Cards for all LGU inventories and observe the periodic
reconciliation of the two records to monitor agency assets properly; (ii) conduct a
complete semi-annual physical count of inventories and submit the corresponding
RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year in accordance with Section
124 of the MNGAs for LGUs; (iii) maintain a Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) to serve as a basis for preparing Journal Entries to record the supplies
and materials issued; and (iv) conform with the implementing guidelines of COA
Circular 2022-004 regarding the issuance of ICS.

5.15 We recommended and the LCE agreed that the Inventory Committee,
Accounting Office and the Property Officer, as the case may be, to: (i) conduct a
complete annual physical count of PPEs and come up with the RPCPPE in accordance
with Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs; (ii) maintain the Property Cards and PPE
Ledger Cards for all LGUs properties and observe the periodic reconciliation of the
two records to monitor agency’s assets properly; (iii) maintain an Inventory and
Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to properly document and track the
unserviceable assets of the Municipality.

6.2 We recommended and the LCE agreed for the Accounting Section maintain
and regularly update the CIPLC for all ongoing construction projects of the
Municipality to ensure efficient asset management and enhance financial reporting
accuracy.

7.11 We reiterate our prior year’s audit recommendation and the LCE agreed to
enjoin the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and maintain SLs per creditor as
necessary to support the balances presented in the GL and financial statements; and
(ii) exhaust all remedies to gather the details and supporting documents needed for
the identification of the amounts of dormant and long-outstanding payables to
facilitate the reversion and/or write-off of such in accordance with the provisions of
COA-DBM JC No. 1, s. 2021 and COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare the corresponding adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts; and (ii) record transactions based on its nature/substance and according to the definition of accounts under the RCALGU; and (b) Municipal Budget Officer to further classify/break-down each Special Purpose Appropriation into different accounts based on the nature of the goods/services to be procured for each program, project, and activity (PPA).

We recommended and the LCE agreed to enjoin the Municipal Accountant to continue to (i) exhaust all remedies to gather the supporting documents needed for the receivables noted; (ii) henceforth, consider the write-off of dormant receivables for more than 10 years in the books of accounts, in accordance with the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016- 005; (iii) continue to identify the reconciling items between the reciprocal accounts Due from/to Other Funds and effect the adjustments in the accounting records, as necessary, to keep the accounts reconciled; and (iv) improve the monitoring of these reciprocal accounts by ensuring that entries made/posted from one account have a corresponding posting of counterpart entries to the other.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to continue to (i) issue demand letters to all concerned AOs for them to liquidate their cash advances by submitting complete and valid settlement documents or refunding applicable portion of the cash advances, as necessary; and (ii) monitor the schedule of cash advances granted to different AOs to know when these cash advances are due for liquidation.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (i) continue upholding its mandate in promoting local development by appropriating 20% of its annual National Tax Allotment (NTA) pursuant to the provisions of the LGC and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2020-1 dated November 4, 2020; (ii) instruct the Accounting and Budget Offices to be more discerning and ensure that only allowable expenses under the JMC are charged to DF; and (ii) direct the Engineering Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) to ensure the timely delivery of the projects for CY 2022 and every budget year, taking into account the legal rudiments of every project.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to instruct the: a) LDRRM Officer to prepare LDRRMFIP annually in its prescribed format which includes specific PPAs funded from LDRRMF; b) Municipal Accountant to continue to (i) make necessary corrections/adjustments in affected inventory and expense accounts; (ii) observe the perpetual method in recognizing inventory and avoid recording outright expense when accounting for relief goods and DRRM supplies; and (iii) discuss and present the minimum required disclosure in the NFS as specified in COA Circular No. 2014-002; c) Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate RAAO for DRRMF; and d) Supply Officer, in coordination with concerned offices, to maintain Stock Cards and prepare and submit the SSMI and Inventory List of Items for DRRM to the Municipal Accountant for recording and adjusting related expense and inventory accounts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) capacitate the GFPS and concerned officials with the mechanisms and processes in mainstreaming GAD perspective in the LGU through training, conferences, symposia administered by the PCW, DILG, or other NGAs directly involved in GAD concerns; (b) instruct the GFPS to (i) continuously update the GAD database; (ii) enhance the GPB and AR by completely filling out the needed qualitative and quantitative aspects of the activities implemented including data on sex-disaggregation pursuant to MCW, as necessary; and (iii) equip themselves with the GeRL/HGDG tool and PIMME to determine how much of the Agency’s actual disbursements for implementing its major programs can be attributed to GAD.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the a) Officers and employees in charge of the implementation of projects funded by other agencies to continue the timely implementation of projects based on the terms and conditions of the SAs to ensure the efficient delivery of public service to the intended beneficiaries; and b) Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to (i) maintain the good practice of prompt liquidation of fund transfers including the immediate refund of unutilized balance of the said funds; and (ii) update the records of all fund transfers from different agencies for proper monitoring and to establish the reliability of the year-end balance of the accounts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the: (a) Accounting Office to (i) ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation and that necessary data/information are provided before payment; (ii) submit the lacking documents as enumerated in this report; (iii) immediately submit the DVs with supporting documents pertaining to the noted transactions; and (iv) secure from the DepEd accountant a certification confirming that all expenses chargeable against the SEF are net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the DepEd budget; (b) Property/Supply Officer to maintain and prepare RIS/ICS/PAR including PLC on items issued to schools so as to establish monitoring and accountability; and (c) BAC to follow the prescribed necessary rules and regulations in the conduct of government procurement.

We also recommended and Management agreed to strive to fully utilize the SEF in accordance with the priority programs/projects to realize their foremost value for the benefit of stakeholders in the education sector.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (i) continue to remit promptly and intact the loan amortization and mandatory contributions to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth within the prescribed period; (ii) exhaust efforts in gathering pertinent documents and analyze the subject accounts to establish the accuracy of the balances in the inter-agency payables; and (iii) remit all the due premiums immediately, when evidence warrants.
We recommended and the LCE concurred that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel enforce the immediate settlement of disallowances in accordance with Section 9.4 of the 2009 RRSA.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the a) Municipal Accountant to (i) make necessary corrections/adjustments in affected inventory and expense accounts; (ii) observe the perpetual method in recognizing inventory and avoid recording outright expense when accounting for relief goods and DRRM supplies; and (b) Supply Officer, in coordination with concerned offices, to maintain SCs and prepare and submit the SSMI and updated Inventory List of Items for DRRM to the Municipal Accountant for recording and adjusting related expense and inventory accounts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) BAC, together with the TWG and Accounting Office, (i) to comply with the stipulated requirements of R.A. No. 9184 and its revised IRR in the procurement activities; and (ii) to submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team; (b) Municipal Accountant to check and ensure the completeness and reliability of documents before effecting payment.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to enjoin the Municipal Accountant to (i) exhaust all means to gather the supporting documents needed to establish the correct amount of receivables noted; (ii) consider writing off receivables which have become dormant for more than 10 years, in accordance with the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2016- 005; (iii) take up only the final and executory audit disallowances and charges in the account Receivables – Disallowances/Charges; (iv) update the records of the account Receivables – Disallowances/Charges and reconcile it with the Audit Team’s records; (v) continue identifying the reconciling items between the reciprocal accounts Due from/to Other Funds and effect the adjustments in the accounting records, as necessary; and (vi) thoroughly monitor reciprocal accounts and ensure that entries posted from one account has a parallel posting to the other counterpart account.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) issue demand letters to all concerned AOs for them to liquidate their cash advances by submitting complete and valid settlement documents or refunding applicable portion of the cash advances, whichever is applicable; (ii) thoroughly monitor the liquidation of cash advances granted to different AOs; and (iii) continue to submit on time the liquidation report for confidential fund cash advance.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) furnish the updated RPTAR/ Taxpayer’s index card to the Municipal Accountant at every beginning of the year for recording  and adjustment purposes; (ii) remit the shares of the Province and the Barangays from the advance RPT collections paid by taxpayers in prior years; (iii) ensure timely remittance of Barangays’ share in RPT within five days after the end of each quarter; and (iv) intensify and strategize tax collection efforts such as deployment of demand letters, posting of notice of delinquency in conspicuous places, and tapping the assistance of the barangay officials during tax campaign to reach out to a larger area; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) establish RPT and SET receivables sourced from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year; (ii) make the necessary adjustments to account the proper treatment of advance tax collections and related discounts in the RPT/SET Receivable and Deferred Income accounts; (iii) adjust the recorded RPT and SET Receivable accounts, after advance collection adjustment, to tally with the total tax actually collectible per certified list as maintained by the Treasury Office; (iv) ensure reversal of deferred income accounts and recognition of the related income accounts made in the current year for the elapsed advance payment collected from prior years; and (v) align the accounting procedures being adopted with the procedures provided by the MNGAS for LGUs with respect to recording of RPT/SET transactions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to enjoin the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and maintain SLs per creditor to support the balances presented in the GL and financial statements; and (ii) exhaust all remedies to gather the details and supporting documents needed for the identification of the amounts of dormant and long-outstanding payables to facilitate the reversion and/or write-off of such in accordance with the provisions of COA-DBM JC No. 1, s. 2021 and COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare the corresponding adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts; (ii) record transactions based on its nature/substance and according to the definition of accounts under the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs; and (b) Municipal Budget Officer to further classify/break-down each Special Purpose Appropriations into different accounts based on the nature of the goods/services to be procured for each PPA.

We recommended and the LCE agreed for (i) the Management to continue upholding its mandate in promoting local development by appropriating 20% of its annual IRA pursuant to the provisions of the Local Government Code and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; and (ii) the Engineering Office and Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) to ensure timely delivery of projects every budget year, taking into account the legal rudiments of every project.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) BAC and concerned officials in charge of procurement to continue to use the Checklist of Requirements and align its contents with the processes and documents established in R.A. No. 9184, infusing the observations noted by the Audit Team; (b) BAC and TWG to submit the lacking documents as enumerated in the discussion; (c) Engineering Office to (i) demand from the contractors Warranty Security before issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance; and (ii) ensure that project contractors observe the proper size and details to be provided on project signboards including the update thereof to reflect the actual project status in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and (d) Accounting Office to check and ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the a) LDRRM Officer to prepare LDRRMFIP annually in its prescribed format which includes specific PPAs funded from LDRRMF current appropriation and from unexpended balance of the previous years transferred to the STF; b) Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and submit the monthly report on sources and utilization of DRRMF covering all funds from local and different sources and from current and continuing appropriation within the reglementary period; and (ii) discuss and present the minimum required disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statments as specified in COA Circular No. 2014-002; and c) Municipal Budget Officer to maintain a separate RAAO for DRRMF for QRF, MOOE and CO.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) direct the MSWMB to (i) to review every two years or as the need arises, the SWMP for the purpose of ensuring its sustainability, viability and effectiveness; (ii) prepare an Annual Work Plan and Budget showing the SWM activities to be implemented and the financial counterpart for each SWM activity, taking into consideration the initiatives identified in the 10-year SWM Plan; and (iii) organize monitoring and evaluation team and require them to prepare Progress Reports in line with the Annual Work Plan and the SWM Plan; (b) consider reinforcing the MENRO to carve out more progress in the implementation of SWMP; and (c) require the cooperation of the Barangays in the implementation of Solid Waste Management particularly the establishment of functional MRF and campaign for proper waste segregation.

We recommended and the (a) LCE agreed to continue to enhance the GFPS and concerned officials with the mechanisms and processes in mainstreaming GAD  perspective in the LGU through trainings, conferences, symposia administered by the PCW, DILG or other NGAs directly involved in GAD concerns; and (b) GFPS agreed to (i) continue to update the GAD database; (ii) enhance the GPB and AR by completely filling out the needed quantitative aspects of the activities implemented including data on sex-disaggregation pursuant to MCW, as necessary; and (iii) equip themselves with the GeRL/HGDG tool and PIMME to determine how much of the Agency’s actual disbursements for implementing its major programs can be attributed to GAD.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the a) Local Finance Committee (LFC) (i) to design a systematic strategy for forecasting revenue target by engaging different Sections/Departments of the LGU such as the BPLO and Assessor’s Office; and (ii) to identify additional sources of income and/or push for the passage of updated revenue code to boost local collection of taxes and fees; b) Municipal Accountant, aiding the LFC and Municipal Treasurer, to lead the correct mapping of revenue account related to the receipt from operation of the eco-tourism park to align it with the revenue categories per Annual Budget for more accurate comparison of the projected and actual income per sources as presented in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amount (SCBAA); and c) Municipal Treasurer to constantly monitor, intensify and strategize collection efforts  by sending payment advisory or notice to taxpayers before the payment period or deadline.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the concerned agency officials to (i) allocate budget for insurance policies covering all insurable properties of the Municipality and to secure said policies from the GSIS so that the agency is protected and indemnified should loss due to calamities arise; and (ii) conduct a complete annual physical count of PPEs and Inventories and come up with the RPCPPE and RPCI and accordingly prepare PIF to be submitted to the Audit Team and GSIS to secure insurance.

We recommended and the LCE agreed, in coordination with the SB, to (i) cause the formal constitution of clear guidelines on the provision of communication allowance by setting the parameters as to eligibility of those who may be qualified to the benefit and the reasonable limit of the monthly entitlement, taking into consideration the value for money packages offered by telecommunication companies vis-à-vis the actual need to carry out official tasks; (ii) ensure the inclusion in the policy guidelines of a provision guaranteeing LGU’s ownership over the mobile phone units in the postpaid plans; and (iii) direct the concerned officials to submit the required certification of official calls and invoice/official receipts as part of the documents to support a claim for reimbursement/payment of communication expense.

We recommended and the LCE concurred with the recommendation that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel enforce the immediate settlement of suspensions and disallowances in accordance with Sections 9.4 of the 2009 RRSA.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct all concerned agency officials and personnel to (i) demand from persons liable the return or refund of the overpayment totaling ₱4,639.80; and (ii) submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team and strictly observe the provisions of the previously mentioned regulations.

We recommended that (a) the heads of each office be mindful of the importance of issuing office order with prescribed work schedule whenever employees are required to report for work on days when they are not supposed to, for the protection of the employees; (b) all concerned agency personnel (i) immediately submit the duly approved DTRs; and (ii) return the excessive claims of hazard pay for March 16, 2020; and (c) the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the HR - Designate, support the disbursement for payroll transactions with adequate documents for the efficient review and validation by the Audit Team and comply with the above provisions.

We recommended and (a) the concerned Municipal officials and employees agreed to prepare all the required reports in a timely manner and submit the same to the concerned oversight government agencies; and (b) the Municipal Accountant agreed to present the minimum required disclosure incorporating a discussion on the quantity received, distributed and balance of donation in-kind in the Notes to the FS to foster compliance to existing rules and promote transparency and accountability in the administration of donations intended to augment the needs of the constituents during the lockdown/quarantine period.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Treasury Office to update the RPTARs/ Taxpayers’ Index Card yearly, to include not only the land owner with updated payment but also those not paying their yearly land tax, and subsequently, prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and (b) Municipal Accountant to establish RPT and SET receivables sourced from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year. Further, we recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Assessor/ Project Manager to coordinate with the Provincial Project Manager for the conduct of training of municipal personnel concerned who are tasked to operate the system so that a test run of the same be conducted immediately to evaluate the efficiency and effectiveness of the PATAS.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct (a) the Inventory Committee to facilitate a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances through the implementation of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated lapsing schedule showing correct computation of depreciation expense that will reconcile with the amounts recorded and presented in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed to maximize the utilization of the 20% DF by fully implementing priority development projects identified pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. We also recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (i) BAC together with the Technical Working Group (TWG) to comply with the aforementioned rules and regulations of R.A.9184; (ii) Accounting Office to check and ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment; and (iii) concerned officials to submit the lacking documents as enumerated in the discussion.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) LDRRM Officer and concerned officials (i) to strictly conform with the provisions in preparing the necessary LDRRMFIP in prescribed format which includes the PPAs funded from LDRRMF current appropriation and from unexpended balance of the previous years transferred to the Special Trust Fund; and (ii) submit justification/explanation as to the legal basis of the disbursement for the grants of meal allowances; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and submit the monthly report on sources and utilization of DRRMF covering all funds from local and different sources and from current and continuing appropriation within the reglementary period; and (ii) discuss and present the minimum required disclosure in the Notes to the FS as specified in COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (i) BAC together with the TWG to comply with the stipulated requirements of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 for emergency procurement activities; (ii) Municipal Accountant to check and ensure the completeness and reliability of documents before effecting payment; and (iii) all concerned agency officials and personnel to submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team and strictly observe the provisions of the abovementioned regulations.

We recommended and the Management agreed to continue to observe the process of incorporating at least 5% of its total Annual Budget for GAD PPAs. We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Treasurer i) deposit the collections intact and regularly with the authorized government depository bank; and ii) require the collectors to remit/ turn over daily the collections to her.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) make representation to the members of the Sangguniang Bayan for the amendment of the affected provision in the Market Code particularly the timeline for the payment of rental fees by stall owners so that policy and practice are synched to each other; and (b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly enforce the terms and conditions provided in the Market Code pending the approval of the proposed amendment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as chairman of the Municipal DRRM Council, cause the selection and accreditation of CSOs and private sector representative in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the aforementioned MC.
